behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the exact file path (packages/solid-router/src/headContentUtils.tsx), the function name (useTags), the import (replaceEqualDeep from @tanstack/router-core), the use of Solid.createMemo with prev parameter, and replaceEqualDeep(prev, next) call — all specifics asserted in the tests.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md section 'Test Requirements' explicitly states the exact fix: import replaceEqualDeep from @tanstack/router-core, return Solid.createMemo with a prev parameter callback, and call replaceEqualDeep(prev, next). This tells the agent exactly WHAT to change, not just what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex to assert exact patterns like 'return Solid.createMemo((prev', 'replaceEqualDeep(prev, next)', and import from '@tanstack/router-core'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. a different variable name (like 'previous' instead of 'prev'), or 'next' named differently, or a different deep-equal utility, or wrapping in a helper function, would fail these regex tests despite being functionally correct.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The three fail-to-pass tests (test_use_tags_imports_replace_equal_deep, test_use_tags_returns_create_memo, test_use_tags_calls_replace_equal_deep) are purely text-matching with regex on file content — they never import, execute, or test actual behavior. While pass-to-pass tests do run builds/tests, the core fail-to-pass tests are all 'assert pattern in open(path).read()'.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub that simply writes the matching text patterns into headContentUtils.tsx (e.g., adding the import line and a function with 'return Solid.createMemo((prev' and 'replaceEqualDeep(prev, next)') without actually fixing the bug could pass the three f2p tests, since they only check for string patterns in the file.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution is in /workspace/router accessible to the agent via solve.sh (which is at solution/solve.sh in the container). The agent could read or cat the solution file. Also, since tests are pure regex on file content, the agent could grep test files to discover exact patterns needed.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with comments into 'FAIL-TO-PASS TESTS' and 'PASS-TO-PASS TESTS' sections, each test has a descriptive name and docstring.
